title: Update working memory from session 606f4961
summary: Durable context from the portal/global-favorites crash investigation and multi-audio playback support session.

flows.name: Expose alternate audio tracks in embedded playback engines

claims.text: Before the session fix, navigating into global favorites could pass `null` as `ChannelListContainerComponent.channelList` before data resolved, and the old displayed-channel computation then tried to filter that null value.

`ChannelListContainerComponent.channelList` now coalesces `null` or `undefined` to an empty array before updating `_channelList`, `channelListSignal`, `channelList$`, and EPG fetching, so downstream channel-list consumers receive an array during transient global-favorites loads.

`VjsPlayerComponent` observes Video.js `audioTracks()` on metadata and track-list events, logs available tracks, and only attempts to show or add the control-bar `audioTrackButton` when Video.js exposes more than one audio track.

`VjsPlayerComponent.logAudioTracks` inspects VHS/HLS AUDIO media groups through `player.tech({ IWillNotUseThisInPlugins: true })` and optional playlist metadata, avoiding direct private `tech_` access while diagnosing missing audio renditions.

`ArtPlayerComponent` listens for `Hls.Events.AUDIO_TRACKS_UPDATED` on m3u8 playback, adds an ArtPlayer settings selector only when HLS.js reports multiple audio tracks, and switches tracks by assigning `hls.audioTrack` from the selected label.

The audio-track selector should stay hidden for streams where the playback engine exposes zero or one audio track; the UI support added in this session targets streams with multiple engine-visible tracks.

A live TV stream may show two audio tracks in VLC or MPV while Video.js/VHS reports only one default track; in that state the current Video.js audio menu will not appear because it depends on `audioTracks().length > 1`.

Investigate Video.js live-TV streams where VLC/MPV can switch multiple audio tracks but `audioTracks()` exposes only one default track, including whether the extra audio is muxed in the transport stream, absent from HLS `EXT-X-MEDIA`, or requires a different Video.js/VHS integration path.

sources.title: Claude Code session 606f4961 portal and audio playback investigation

metadata.reason: The assistant diagnosis in the transcript explains that global-favorites navigation passed null into the channel-list input before data resolved, causing the displayed-channel computation to filter null.
The implementation summary stated that the audio menu should appear only when the stream contains multiple audio tracks and remain invisible for single-track streams.
The user supplied logs showing Video.js reported one default audio track while the same stream exposed two switchable audio tracks in VLC or MPV.
The final user message left unresolved why Video.js saw only one track for a stream where VLC and MPV exposed two tracks.
